events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes Amid Rising Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran exchanged missile strikes, with the US targeting Iranian missile sites and command centers, and Iran claiming to have attacked US bases. This escalation follows recent attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and raises concerns about wider regional instability.

events.title: Iranian Forces Claim to Strike US Military Assets in Multiple Gulf Allies
events.desc: Following US strikes, Iranian forces announced they had targeted US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman. This broadens the conflict theater and increases the risk of direct confrontation between Iran and US allies.

events.title: Chemical Tanker Explodes Off Omani Coast Amid Regional Tensions
events.desc: The chemical tanker Stolt Magnesium caught fire after an explosion attributed to an unidentified external device in the Arabian Sea off Oman. While the crew was safe, the incident adds to concerns about maritime security in the region, which is already heightened due to US-Iran hostilities.

events.title: Russia Accuses Ukraine of Terrorism Over Sea of Azov Drone Attacks
events.desc: Russia has accused Ukraine of terrorism following overnight drone attacks on 11 Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov. Ukraine claims to have struck five tankers, five dry cargo vessels, and a tugboat, escalating conflict along a crucial grain export route.

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills 10 Palestinians in Gaza, Including Child and Police Officer
events.desc: An Israeli strike and gunfire in the Gaza Strip resulted in the deaths of at least 10 Palestinians, including a 10-year-old boy and a Hamas police officer. This incident further exacerbates the ongoing conflict and humanitarian crisis in the region.

events.title: Israel Approves Budget for 34 New West Bank Settlements
events.desc: Israel's security cabinet approved a significant budget to establish 34 new settlements in the occupied West Bank. This decision is expected to further escalate tensions in the region and is viewed by international bodies as an obstacle to peace.

events.title: Houthi Leader Threatens 'Siege' on Saudi Arabia After Yemen Airport Attack
events.desc: A leading Houthi figure has threatened a 'siege' on Saudi Arabia in retaliation for alleged strikes on Yemen's capital. This threat escalates regional tensions and raises concerns about further destabilization in the Arabian Peninsula.

events.title: Sudan Faces Escalating Hunger Crisis Due to War and Hormuz Disruption
events.desc: The World Food Programme warns that Sudan is at risk of a deeper hunger crisis. The ongoing conflict, coupled with aid funding cuts and rising agricultural costs linked to the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, threatens to reverse previous gains in food security.

events.title: US Consumer Prices Drop, but Renewed Tensions With Iran Could Revive Inflation
events.desc: A decrease in US consumer prices due to falling energy costs has eased inflation concerns. However, experts warn that the renewed tensions and attacks involving Iran could lead to a resurgence of price increases.

events.title: Trump Backtracks on Hormuz Toll Plan, Pivots to Gulf Investment Deals
events.desc: President Donald Trump has reversed his plan to impose a 20% levy on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Instead, he aims to secure compensation through trade deals with Gulf allies, signaling a shift in US strategy amid heightened tensions with Iran.

events.title: Return to US-Iran Hostilities a 'Huge Setback' for Civilians, UN Rights Chief States
events.desc: The UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, Volker Turk, has declared the resumption of hostilities between the US and Iran a significant blow to civilians in the region. He warned of severe disruptions to essential supplies and increased instability.

events.title: Trump Hosts Iraqi PM, Emphasizes Potential Economic Deals Amid Intensifying Iran Conflict
events.desc: President Donald Trump met with Iraqi Prime Minister Zaidi, highlighting Iraq's oil reserves and potential deals with US companies. This meeting occurs as the conflict with Iran intensifies, underscoring Iraq's strategic importance.

events.title: Cuban Power Grid Collapses Amid US Oil Blockade
events.desc: Cuba experienced its fifth nationwide blackout of 2026 due to a collapse of its power grid, reportedly exacerbated by a US-imposed oil blockade. This outage affects millions and highlights the impact of external sanctions on critical infrastructure.

events.title: Iran Turning to Red Sea Gateway as New Pressure Point After Hormuz Blockade
events.desc: Following actions impacting the Strait of Hormuz, Iran is reportedly leveraging its Houthi allies to threaten the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the Red Sea. This strategic move aims to create a new pressure point against the US and its allies, endangering vital energy routes.

events.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps
events.desc: The UK has announced a ban on support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), citing its links to death threats and intimidation on British soil. This move reflects growing international pressure on Iran.

events.title: Israeli Attack on North Gaza Police Station Kills Eight
events.desc: An Israeli attack on a police station in northern Gaza has resulted in the deaths of eight individuals, including police personnel and a civilian. Gaza's interior ministry condemned the incident as a 'horrific massacre'.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis Launch Missiles at Saudi Arabia After Strikes on Sanaa Airport
events.desc: Following reported strikes on Sanaa airport, Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis have launched missiles targeting Saudi Arabia, specifically Abha airport. This retaliatory action escalates the conflict in the region.
